Guests have included Stacey Solomon, Lisa Faulkner, Tara Reid, Rufus Wainwright and Marina Diamandis.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: In the penultimate leg, the race shifts up a gear as the four remaining teams enter their eighth and final country, Mongolia. Facing freezing temperatures and rugged off-road travel, the intrepid duos set out on an expedition across the endless steppe and deep into the unknown, traversing landscapes few travellers have ever experienced. It tests every ounce of their resilience as they battle to take pole position at the final checkpoint before the finish line.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: The Theory of Everything
programme.desc: During the 1960s, Cambridge University student and future physicist Stephen Hawking falls in love with arts student Jane Wilde. Their relationship leads him through personal and scientific challenges.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: See Hear explores how technology is transforming life for deaf people, from communication tools and AI to new ways of connecting, working and living in a digital world. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined in the studio by Billy-Jack Gerrard, who is passionate about AI and how it is shaping the future. He explains how AI works, how coding underpins everyday technology and how his own experiences as a deaf coder have influenced his AI career path and thinking.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the FBI, a quiet Hungarian immigrant landscaper, with no apparent ties to organised crime and no known record, concocts an elaborate extortion plan.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: One patient's rare penis cancer requires a partial amputation, and another patient with severe endometriosis must have a huge cyst and a damaged kidney removed.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his journey at the tense frontier of the Demilitarised Zone before heading to the capital of Seoul, a vast-fast moving city that has become one of the world's most advanced cities.

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Working away every week had dad Simon exhausted and put a strain on his relationship with his wife, Helen. She believed a fresh start in Australia could be the solution, but leaving the UK would mean Helen having to say goodbye to her mum.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A major crack in a two-bedroom cottage in New Malden does not stop a fashion student from buying it or from giving the finished project the wow factor. Meanwhile, seasoned developers in Leeds and Greater Manchester tackle issues in their terraces.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: Retired couple Steven and Sue love their two-bedroom bungalow, but Steven's mobility issues have left Sue taking on more of the workload, and the tired, cluttered shed is making things difficult. The team need to turn their shed into a practical and welcoming space for Sue to do her volunteer work, and make it easier for Steven to access his mobility scooter.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: Lion numbers continue to grow, but there's not enough food to go around; a lioness mum risks her life to feed her five cubs; Xudum's newborn leopard cubs face a deadly threat.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The residents of Whitchurch in Shropshire turn up a treasure trove of items, including a valuable pair of wooden peat buckets, a pair of silver claret jugs crafted by one of the greatest silversmiths of all time, and a collection of snuff boxes and candlesticks valued at £75,000.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Some of the best unseen finds from the show's valuations days from around the country, including visits to the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, Ragley Hall in Warwickshire, the National Maritime Museum Cornwall in Falmouth and the RAF museum in Hendon, London.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo Williams explores the Tywi river valley, from Llansteffan's coastal dunes to fallow deer ruts, ancient oak woodlands, and the remote heights of the Tywi Forest.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah embarks on a journey to investigate her own biological age and whether it can really be controlled. On the island of Okinawa in Japan, she sees the key to some of the healthiest old women in the world.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah Fry has a look at the bank card. This humble piece of plastic is often taken for granted, but it's the digital key to accessing cash and is packed full of extraordinary technological innovations.

programme.title: The Elon Musk Show
programme.desc: By 2016, Elon Musk is moving fast with several new business ideas: building underground tunnels, creating chips that can be inserted into people's brains and attempting to build the biggest battery factory the world has ever seen.

programme.title: Children of the Blitz

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Meticulous and methodical, Big John engineers his ransom scheme with military precision. On paper, it's flawless. But as the deadline approaches, nothing goes according to plan, and two battles unfold simultaneously: a high-stakes ransom exchange and a desperate race to stop a bomb designed to be unstoppable. Precision planning meets a chaotic reality, and one miscalculation on either side could mean catastrophe.

programme.title: MasterChef

programme.title: Interior Design Masters

The charity's ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers before Michelle and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces.

programme.title: The Cage
programme.desc: Matty flails as Ning continues to push him. Meanwhile, Leanne and Gary bond, until he pressures Leanne to betray Matty to safeguard her family.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in stunning south-west Wales, helping Carol, along with her husband, return to the country where she grew up. With her son Greg supporting her on the house hunt, she's dreaming of a home close to the sea. The property line-up includes a picture-perfect double-fronted house with roses around the door, and a mystery house boasting spectacular mountain views... it's Wales at its most wonderful.
